image.alt: Photographic portrait of a stern-faced British statesman in a suit

rounds.revealFact: Winston Churchill served as British Prime Minister during World War II and again in the early 1950s.

image.alt: Medieval illustration of armored knights and archers clashing in a muddy field

attribution.fileTitle: File:Battle of Agincourt, St. Alban's Chronicle by Thomas Walsingham.jpg
attribution.author: Unknown, English School (15th century)

clues: A vastly outnumbered army of longbowmen and men-at-arms fought in a narrow field hemmed in by woods.
Heavy overnight rain had turned the ground to thick mud that trapped charging horses and armored knights.

events.label: The Louisiana Purchase roughly doubles the size of the United States
The first photograph of a human being is captured on a Paris street
The Suez Canal opens, connecting the Mediterranean and Red Seas
Marconi transmits the first transatlantic radio signal

image.alt: A vast crowd of people seated outdoors on a grassy field stretching to the horizon

attribution.fileTitle: File:Looking toward the back of the field at Woodstock.jpg
attribution.author: James M Shelley

rounds.prompt: Nelson Mandela walks free after 27 years in prison.
rounds.revealFact: Mandela was released after decades of imprisonment and became South Africa's first Black president in 1994.

rounds.prompt: Where did French kings build an enormous palace just outside their capital?
rounds.revealFact: Louis XIV moved the royal court to Versailles in 1682, and its Hall of Mirrors later hosted the treaty that ended World War I.
